tests.intent: Pinned TypeScript strict no-emit compilation and evaluator lint reject implicit any, unchecked DOM/null paths, suppression directives, debug statements, generated bundles, and non-TypeScript source modules.

Client source contains only API, input, codec, palette, canvas, audio, presentation-state, and bootstrap modules with no framework, engine, simulation, SQL, evaluator, test, Node, remote, dynamic-code, worker, WASM, or forbidden import.

Navigation immediately presents one visible intrinsic 320 by 200 game canvas as the dominant viewport content, without splash, consent, marketing, landing, loading-button, or delayed start interaction.

Evaluator-owned gzip, canonical column-RLE, palette-index, row-major RGBA, frame identity, malformed-run, truncation, extra-column, and palette-range fixtures are decoded independently of all client code.

Raw canvas getImageData bytes match exact independent RGBA SHA-256 identities at initial and successive deterministic frames, proving palette lookup, orientation, alpha, decode, and complete replacement.
A deterministic desktop screenshot and geometry probe show a crisp aspect-preserving canvas centered at maximal scale with controls unobtrusive, no overlap, no clipping, and no document overflow.

Portrait and landscape screenshots plus geometry probes show a maximal aspect-preserving canvas, reachable minimal icon controls, safe hit targets, no overlap, clipping, scrollbars, text labels, or viewport escape.

Keyboard down/up and repeat behavior covers movement, turning, fire, use, automap, menu, pause, and audio while exact public command envelopes remain bounded, sequenced, integer-valued, and free of stuck controls.

Pointer and touch press, hold, release, cancel, slide-away, simultaneous movement plus action, toggles, and visibility changes produce exact commands without duplicate firing, stuck state, synthetic keyboard events, or page gestures.

Audio begins only after a user gesture, loads allowlisted assets through the API, schedules each server audio tuple once using gain and pan fields, supports mute, and never synthesizes gameplay state or treats decode failure as success.

Every browser run rejects console errors, page errors, failed requests, HTTP errors, unexpected routes, cross-origin traffic, redirects, external assets, duplicate bootstrap calls, and swallowed malformed payload failures.
Presentation state owns only loading, mode, mute, focus, and visibility concerns; losing focus or page visibility releases held inputs and never advances simulation locally or predicts a frame.

Focused client mutations, positive and surviving canaries, evaluator self-attacks, immutable ancestry pointers, fixture/reference hashes, exact ids, and visual-boundary metadata all fail closed before implementation.
